programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: Key Witness Under Fire In Cross-Examination
programme.desc: Jennifer McCabe shares her version of what happened, as they found John O'Keefe in the snow.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: Karen Read's attorney had more questions for one of the State's key witnesses, Jennifer McCabe.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: Karen Read's defense tried to discredit the State's key witness. According to Jennifer McCabe, the defendant was the one who admitted that she hit her boyfriend, John O'Keefe, with her car. The Defense brought up the 911 call McCabe made that morning. Before McCabe took the stand, no one else had claimed that read and O'Keefe got into a fight before he died. This aligned with the State's allegation that the defendant killed him because their relationship became strained.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jennifer McCabe told jurors that Karen Read's SUV had a cracked taillight. She was on the stand testifying about what happened when she and Karen Read got to the scene and eventually discovered John O'Keefe in the snow. Prosecutors believe the defendant used that car to hit him and then left him to die. During this long cross-examination, McCabe denied deleting text messages and phone logs in the days after O'Keefe's death.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: The sparring between Karen Read's attorney and the prosecution's key witness was not over yet.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: The death of John O'Keefe took a mental toll on the State's key witness. Jennifer McCabe was with the defendant when they found the victim in the snow. She was the one who called 911 to try to save him. The Defense tried to poke holes in her testimony by asking her a number of accusatory questions asserting that she is covering up what happened to her friend, O'Keefe. McCabe told the jury: I never would have left John O'Keefe out in the cold to die because he was my friend that I loved.

programme.desc: A convicted killer serving life for manslaughter is found to be guilty of a second murder 32 years later.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Westley developed predatory urges as a teen, escalating his crimes over the years. By 28, he murdered three young boys before a failed attempt to take another child exposed him and ended his deadly spree.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.desc: Two criminals, who met in prison while serving time for previous murders, struck up a friendship, which had deadly consequences.
